summary: Verified the NEW mobile behaviour in Banner.jsx — mobile text now hidden by default and tap-reveals (concept-parity with desktop hover), and the custom glasses cursor is hidden on mobile. All 6 spec points pass with zero console errors.

mobile_390x844.panel_mobile_text_initial: 9 elements, all display:block with opacity=0 (hidden by default) — PASS
mobile_390x844.tap_to_reveal: Clicked centred panel (.group inner wrapper of the middle carousel-panel, idx=4 title '127'); its [data-testid=panel-mobile-text] opacity transitioned 0 -> 1; panel-title-mobile='127' and panel-excerpt-mobile starts with 'Vanuit de dagelijkse praktijk ontstond het idee voor 127.be: een online platform dat een brede verzameling IT-tools samenbrengt op één centrale plaats...' — PASS
mobile_390x844.tap_to_hide: Second click on the same inner wrapper -> panels with opacity=1 collapses to [] — PASS
mobile_390x844.sweep: Circular sweep still active on mobile (verified in prior iteration; not regressed — panel positions/transforms unchanged this run)

desktop_1920x800.hover_reveal: Hovering the centred tile makes [data-testid=panel-title-wrap] opacity=1 showing 'RADIOGROEP' and [data-testid=panel-excerpt-wrap] opacity=1 showing 'Voor Radiogroep was Yannick Gijbels actief als Broadcast Engineer, waarbij hij verantwoordelijk was voor de technische o…' — PASS

verification_details.console: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 pageerrors on both viewports

critical_code_review_comments: handlePanelSelect gates on window.innerWidth<768 — correct, and matches handleMouseMove branch. Consider a shared useIsMobile hook + resize listener to avoid reading innerWidth per event; not a functional issue.

Because loopItems triples the articles (3 copies), tapping panel idx=4 in the middle copy is the intended UX (only that single loop-index toggles). Tapping the same article in another copy (idx=1 or 7) will NOT stay in sync — activeIdx is loop-index based, not article-id based. Low priority since the visible tile is always the centred one.

Panel wrapper has both onMouseEnter (fires fetchBody) and onClick (toggles active). On touch devices some browsers synthesise mouseenter before click, which currently just triggers fetchBody again (deduped) — no side-effect. OK.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mobile tap-reveal fully working: panel-mobile-text now starts opacity:0 (display:block) and toggles to opacity:1 on click of the .group inner wrapper inside the centred [data-testid=carousel-panel]. Custom cursor is display:none on mobile via `hidden ... sm:block` classes; handleMouseMove also sets opacity=0 defensively on mobile. Desktop unchanged: hover reveals floating title/body, custom cursor visible & follows mouse. To test on mobile, click the centred tile's inner wrapper (not the outer carousel-panel container) via JS since transformed elements sometimes intercept mouse.click at odd coords. Preview base = https://saffron-header.preview.emergentagent.com.
rca of the issue: Not a bug — feature verification. Implementation change (Panel gained active/onSelect props; mobile-text wrapper uses `${active?'opacity-100':'opacity-0'}`; handlePanelSelect toggles activeIdx only when innerWidth<768; custom cursor uses `hidden ... sm:block` + handleMouseMove opacity gate) all behave exactly as specified.
